annotations.description: Verifies that packet classes correctly determine whether they are an LE variant when dispatching to child payload classes. The detection method must work for both the base class and its LE variant.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 125-132 (IRemoteSCMActivator), 274-280 (OpcDaAuth3), 539-545 (OpcDaHeaderN): Changes self.__name__.endswith('LE') to self.__class__.__name__.endswith('LE') in guess_payload_class methods
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Some packet classes fail to correctly identify whether they are an LE variant when dispatching to child packets'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Python instances don't have a __name__ attribute; only classes do. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must fix the LE variant detection in packet dispatch methods.

title: 1.1: Fixes LE variant detection in all 3 guess_payload_class methods (IRemoteSCMActivator_RemoteCreateInstance, OpcDaAuth3, OpcDaHeaderN) in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that the PDU type dispatch dictionary correctly maps PDU types to their LE variant classes. Entries that incorrectly reference the base class instead of the LE variant must be fixed.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 515-516, 520-521: Changes OpcDaFack to OpcDaFackLE and OpcDaBind_nak to OpcDaBind_nakLE in _opcDa_pdu_classes dictionary
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The PDU type dispatch dictionary also has entries that incorrectly reference the big-endian variant for PDU types 9 (Fack) and 13 (Bind_nak) when an LE variant should be used'. [3: Logical Necessity] Incorrect dispatch entries cause wrong class selection when parsing LE packets. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must fix the PDU type dispatch dictionary entries.

title: 1.2: Fixes both LE variant class entries (OpcDaFackLE and OpcDaBind_nakLE) in the PDU type dispatch dictionary in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that field name typos in packet classes are corrected: 'Length' misspellings in OpcDaHeaderN, 'pda2' to 'pad2' in OpcDaBind_ack/OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp, and 'portSPec' to 'portSpec' in OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 532-535 (OpcDaHeaderN): fragLenght to fragLength, authLenght to authLength. Lines 446-447 (OpcDaBind_ack): pda2 to pad2. Lines 480-481 (OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp): portSPec to portSpec, pda2 to pad2
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'there are also typos in some header field names where Length is misspelled as Lenght, and other minor spelling errors in field names across several packet classes like OpcDaHeaderN and OpcDaBind_ack'. The prompt also states OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp 'should match the structure of similar response packets like OpcDaBind_ack'. [4: Quality Necessity] Consistent and correctly spelled field names improve code maintainability. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must use 'pad2' (not 'pda2') in both OpcDaBind_ack and OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp.

title: 1.3: Fixes all 4 field name typos (fragLenght to fragLength, authLenght to authLength, pda2 to pad2, portSPec to portSpec) in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that the RequestSubData class is renamed to RequestStubData to align with RPC stub data terminology, as specified in the prompt interface.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 283-306: Renames class RequestSubData to RequestStubData and RequestSubDataLE to RequestStubDataLE
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The RequestSubData class should be renamed to RequestStubData' to align with RPC stub data conventions. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename the RequestSubData class to RequestStubData.

title: 1.4: Renames RequestSubData class to RequestStubData in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that the subData field is renamed to stubData in packet classes that contain it (such as OpcDaRequest, OpcDaResponse, OpcDaFault, OpcDaReject).
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 335-336 (OpcDaRequest), 359-361 (OpcDaResponse), 379-381 (OpcDaFault), 398-400 (OpcDaReject): Renames subData field to stubData
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all subData fields should be renamed to stubData'. [4: Quality Necessity] Consistent naming with RPC stub data conventions improves code clarity. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename all subData fields to stubData.

title: 1.5: Renames subData field to stubData in all 4 packet classes (OpcDaRequest, OpcDaResponse, OpcDaFault, OpcDaReject) in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that the LenStringPacket class uses a MultipleTypeField instead of duplicate ConditionalFields for the data field. The field should use a fixed 2-byte field when length is 0, and a variable-length field otherwise.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 326-332: Replaces two ConditionalFields with MultipleTypeField containing StrFixedLenField for length=0 and StrLenField otherwise
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The LenStringPacket class incorrectly uses two ConditionalFields for the same data field name. This should be replaced with a MultipleTypeField'. [3: Logical Necessity] Having two ConditionalFields for the same field name causes ambiguous field access. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must refactor LenStringPacket to use MultipleTypeField.

title: 1.6: Refactors LenStringPacket in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py to use MultipleTypeField for the data field

annotations.description: Verifies that the ResultElement class uses big-endian ShortEnumField for the reason field instead of little-endian LEShortEnumField.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 363-364: Changes LEShortEnumField to ShortEnumField for the reason field in ResultElement
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The ResultElement class incorrectly uses a little-endian field type (LEShortEnumField) for the reason field even though it's a big-endian packet class. It should use ShortEnumField instead'. [3: Logical Necessity] Using wrong endianness causes incorrect parsing of the reason field. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must fix ResultElement to use ShortEnumField for reason.

title: 1.7: Fixes ResultElement endianness in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py by using ShortEnumField for the reason field

annotations.description: Verifies that the AttributeName class is renamed to AV_PAIR with fields renamed to match MS-NLMP specification (avID, avLen, value).
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 649-661: Renames AttributeName to AV_PAIR and renames fields attributeItemType to avID, attributeItemLen to avLen, attributeItem to value
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The AttributeName class should be renamed to AV_PAIR with fields avID, avLen, and value'. [4: Quality Necessity] Field names matching the MS-NLMP specification improve code clarity and maintainability. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rename AttributeName to AV_PAIR with correct field names.

title: 1.8: Renames AttributeName class to AV_PAIR in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py with MS-NLMP field names (avID, avLen, value)

annotations.description: Verifies that OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp has the missing resultList and authentication fields to match OpcDaBind_ack structure.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 482-483: Adds resultList and authentication fields to OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp is missing required fields... It should match the structure of similar response packets like OpcDaBind_ack'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] OpcDaBind_ack has resultList and authentication fields that OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp lacks. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must add fields to match OpcDaBind_ack structure.

title: 1.9: Adds both required fields (resultList and authentication) to OpcDaAlter_Context_Resp in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that the OpcDaRequest UUID field is conditional on the objectUuid flag in the header, not unconditionally present.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 786-791: Wraps uuid field in ConditionalField that checks pfc_flags for objectUuid
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The UUID field in OpcDaRequest should only be present when the objectUuid flag is set in the header, not unconditionally'. [3: Logical Necessity] Unconditional UUID field causes incorrect parsing when objectUuid flag is not set. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must make the UUID field conditional.

title: 1.10: Makes OpcDaRequest UUID field conditional in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py based on objectUuid flag

annotations.description: Verifies that OpcDaFault has an authentication field like other similar packets such as OpcDaReject.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 848-849: Adds PacketField for authentication to OpcDaFault
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The OpcDaFault packet is missing an authentication field that other similar packets like OpcDaReject have. It should have a PacketField for authentication'. [3: Logical Necessity] Missing authentication field causes incomplete packet parsing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must add authentication field to OpcDaFault.

title: 1.11: Adds authentication field to OpcDaFault in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Verifies that _make_le ensures LE variant fields produce correct byte packing after the format string is changed from big-endian to little-endian.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch line 282: Adds f.struct = struct.Struct(f.fmt) after modifying the format string
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'When creating LE variants, the struct object needs to be rebuilt after changing the format string'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Python struct objects cache the format; changing fmt without rebuilding struct causes incorrect packing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must rebuild the struct object in _make_le.

title: 1.12: Rebuilds the cached struct object after changing the format string in _make_le in scapy/contrib/opc_da.py

annotations.description: Detects if OpcDaHeaderN's field definitions are modified beyond the required typo renames. Changes to field count, field types, or field order would alter the wire format for header packets.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch lines 532-535: Only renames fragLenght to fragLength and authLenght to authLength in OpcDaHeaderN without changing field types, order, or count
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only'. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] In Scapy, fields_desc directly determines wire format because each field's type and order maps to specific byte positions in the serialized packet. [3: Logical Necessity] OpcDaHeaderN is the only packet class requiring field modifications (typo fixes), and structural changes beyond renaming would break header parsing. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must NOT modify OpcDaHeaderN fields_desc beyond the required typo renames.

title: 4.1: Modifies OpcDaHeaderN fields_desc beyond correcting field name typos (such as adding, removing, or reordering fields)

annotations.description: Detects if any packet class used in the PDU dispatch dictionary is deleted. These classes are essential for packet type dispatch and must be preserved.
annotations.evidence: Reference patch preserves all PDU packet classes (OpcDaRequest, OpcDaFack, OpcDaBind_nak, etc.) while only fixing dispatch dictionary values
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires fixing PDU dispatch dictionary entries, which depend on the referenced classes existing. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing a class from _opcDa_pdu_classes would cause KeyError or dispatch failures. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, any correct answer must NOT remove packet classes referenced in the PDU dispatch dictionary.

title: 4.2: Removes a packet class referenced in _opcDa_pdu_classes (such as OpcDaFack, OpcDaBind_nak, or their LE variants)
